task_completion.description: Draw the outer rectangular frame of the bookcase.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rectangle is drawn on the canvas in the SolveSpace application.

task_completion.description: Constrain the outer frame to 600 mm wide and 1800 mm high.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Dimension constraints are visible on the outer rectangle, explicitly showing '600' for the width and '1800' for the height.
Exactly 4 horizontal line segments are drawn inside the outer rectangular frame.

task_completion.description: Constrain the 4 interior shelves to be perfectly equally spaced.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Explicit dimension constraints of '360' are visible between each shelf and the top/bottom borders, OR equal-length constraints are applied to vertical construction lines separating the shelves.

task_completion.description: Save the parametric model as bookcase.slvs in the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save' dialog shows the file being saved as 'bookcase.slvs' in '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/', or the SolveSpace window title updates to show this filename.

task_completion.description: Export the 2D view as a DXF file to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export 2D View' or similar export dialog is visible, saving the file as 'bookcase.dxf' in '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/'.

integrity.description: The agent used the SolveSpace graphical user interface to create the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the SolveSpace canvas, toolbars, and constraint menus, rather than terminal commands or text editors.

integrity.description: The agent built the model from scratch step-by-step.
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory shows the progressive addition of lines, rectangles, and constraints over multiple steps, rather than opening a pre-existing completed file.

integrity.description: The agent exported the DXF file using the application's export functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace export dialog is used to generate the DXF, rather than a terminal command like 'touch bookcase.dxf' being used to create a fake file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The bookcase must be exactly 600 mm wide and 1800 mm high. It must contain exactly 4 interior horizontal shelves. The shelves must be equally spaced, resulting in a gap of exactly 360 mm between each shelf (1800 mm / 5 spaces). The model must be saved as '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/bookcase.slvs' and exported as '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/bookcase.dxf'.
